glossary.def: A multivariate time-series model in which each of K variables is regressed on the lagged values of all K variables (itself included), so the coefficient matrices encode mutual feedback and lead-lag structure.
A rewrite of a VAR(p) as an equivalent VAR(1) on a stacked state vector; the VAR is stationary exactly when every eigenvalue of its companion matrix lies strictly inside the unit circle.

The time path of a system's variables following a one-off unit shock to one equation; for a VAR it equals the moving-average coefficients Psi_i (Psi_i = A1^i for a VAR(1)) and turns a coefficient matrix into an interpretable dynamic story.
The share of each variable's h-step-ahead forecast-error variance attributable to each (orthogonalized) structural shock; like the IRF it depends on the identifying ordering imposed on the shocks.

A property of two or more individually I(1) (random-walk) series for which some linear combination beta'y is stationary (I(0)); the series share a common stochastic trend and cannot drift apart without bound.

A differenced VAR augmented with a level term Pi*y_{t-1}, where Pi = alpha*beta' has rank equal to the cointegration rank; the term alpha*(beta'y) pulls the system back toward long-run equilibrium at the speed-of-adjustment loadings alpha.
The number r of linearly independent cointegrating vectors, equal to the rank of the matrix Pi in a VECM (0 <= r < K); estimated by the Johansen trace and maximum-eigenvalue likelihood-ratio tests.

A statement of predictive content: x Granger-causes y if past values of x improve the forecast of y beyond y's own past, tested by an F-test comparing restricted and unrestricted equations. It is not a claim of structural or mechanistic causation.

refs.cite: Sims, C. A. (1980). Macroeconomics and Reality. Econometrica 48(1).
Engle, R. F. & Granger, C. W. J. (1987). Co-integration and Error Correction: Representation, Estimation, and Testing. Econometrica 55(2).
Granger, C. W. J. (1969). Investigating Causal Relations by Econometric Models and Cross-spectral Methods. Econometrica 37(3).
Johansen, S. (1991). Estimation and Hypothesis Testing of Cointegration Vectors in Gaussian Vector Autoregressive Models. Econometrica 59(6).
Lutkepohl, H. (2005). New Introduction to Multiple Time Series Analysis. Springer.
Toda, H. Y. & Yamamoto, T. (1995). Statistical Inference in Vector Autoregressions with Possibly Integrated Processes. Journal of Econometrics 66(1-2).
